epics.title: Move theme guidance from CSS baseline variables into workflow protocol

tasks.title: Document the theme usage protocol
acceptance_criteria: A design-rule-engine document explains how web work selects and records a profile/register.
The protocol explicitly treats aesthetic-profile.json as guidance, not CSS token output.
The protocol explains when runtime CSS token changes are allowed.
done_definition: specs/design-rule-engine/theme-usage.md exists and is linked from README/workflow docs.

tasks.title: Require web agents to read the protocol
acceptance_criteria: website-workflow and design-rule-engine skills list theme-usage.md as a required local read.
Design Agent, Builder, Verifier, and Design Critic prompts include the protocol when rendered web UI is in scope.
AGENTS.md says webpage edits must record the chosen profile/register in run artifacts.
done_definition: Future website changes have a documented path to consult the theme profile without relying on globals.css.

tasks.title: Remove custom theme baseline variables and custom type classes
acceptance_criteria: src/app/globals.css no longer defines --tone-*, --space-*, --leading-*, --tracking-*, --font-body/display/code, or type-* custom typography classes.
No TSX source references the removed CSS variables or type-* classes.
Rendered pages continue using shadcn/Tailwind semantic utilities and local Tailwind classes.
done_definition: Search confirms removed custom CSS hooks are absent from src.
Visual before/after evidence exists for /songs, a song detail page, and /quiz.

tasks.title: Verify browser, lint, and AI-team state
acceptance_criteria: Browser verification captures after screenshots and checks for error overlays.
Verification explicitly checks the served CSS no longer contains removed custom selectors or variables.
done_definition: Verifier, Design Critic, and Code Critic artifacts approve the change or list concrete blockers.

assumptions: The user wants the custom theme baseline removed from CSS, not shadcn/Tailwind runtime variables required by the app.
Visual appearance may become closer to the default shadcn semantic palette after removing repo-specific tone variables.
The existing theme-profile-refresh run remains part of the same branch context.
risks: Migrating away from CSS variables can alter page tone and contrast; browser verification is required.
If any removed variable remains in TSX, pages can render invalid CSS values.
The branch is behind origin/main but has local task changes, so it should not be auto-rebased during this edit.
